content:
---
title: "Exception-based digest design: silence when things are fine, a ping when something is off"
description: "Corporate executives do not want daily emails saying everything is fine; digests skip delivery when there are no anomalies"
date: 2026-03-14
group: zk
type: note
id: "20260314-exception-based-digest-design"
links:
  - "20260314-report-intelligence-highest-value-corporate-wedge"
  - "20260314-single-pane-communication-intelligence-layer"
  - "20260314-shoulder-tapping-tax"
  - "20260314-corporate-property-communication-fault-line"
source: "projects/autoproxy/TECHNICAL_SPEC_V2"
created_by: agent
---

The executive digest feature in AutoProxy follows an exception-based design philosophy: the default is silence, not noise. The `exception_only` flag on DigestConfig is the key design decision -- when enabled, the system generates the digest, checks for exceptions, and if there are none, skips delivery entirely.

This is counter to how most reporting tools work. Traditional dashboards and reports deliver on a schedule regardless of content. The regional manager gets 8 property reports every morning whether or not anything requires attention. The COO gets a 20-slide deck every Monday whether the metrics changed or not. This contributes to the very communication overload that AutoProxy is designed to reduce.

The exception-based approach inverts this: generate digest -> check for anomalies -> if `exception_only` is true and no anomalies exist, skip delivery. The executive receives silence when things are fine and a focused alert when something is off.

Anomaly detection uses a hybrid approach: statistical first (z-score > 2.0 on 30-day rolling averages, Elixir-native), then LLM narration for detected anomalies. The LLM receives the metric name, the anomalous value, the historical range, and any correlated anomalies, then produces a 1-2 sentence explanation and recommended action. This avoids the cost and latency of sending all metrics through an LLM while getting natural language explanations for the anomalies that matter.

The digest structure is configurable per user with sections: exceptions, summary, action items, metrics, pattern alerts, and staff highlights. Daily digests run at the user's preferred time in their timezone; weekly digests on their preferred day. The token budget is constrained: ~2,000 tokens input, ~1,000 tokens output for daily; double for weekly.

The design principle: the highest-value communication tool is the one that respects your attention by only using it when something genuinely deserves it.
